meaning_en: to achieve something successfully
example: The team accomplished all project goals ahead of schedule.

meaning_en: free from errors; exactly correct
example: Please ensure all figures in the report are accurate.

meaning_en: to confirm receipt or awareness of something
example: Please acknowledge receipt of this email by end of day.

meaning_en: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting
example: The agenda for tomorrow's meeting has been sent to all participants.

meaning_en: to distribute resources for a specific purpose
example: The company allocated additional funds for marketing this quarter.

meaning_en: to expect or prepare for something in advance
example: We anticipate strong demand for the new product line.

meaning_en: to officially agree to or accept something
example: The board approved the merger proposal last Friday.

meaning_en: to organize or make plans for something
example: Could you arrange a meeting with the client for next week?

meaning_en: to evaluate or judge the quality or value of something
example: We need to assess the risks before proceeding with the investment.

meaning_en: to give a task or responsibility to someone
example: The manager assigned the new project to our team.

meaning_en: to give official permission for something
example: Only the director is authorized to approve expenses over $5,000.

meaning_en: able to be used or obtained; free to do something

meaning_en: an advantage or profit gained from something

meaning_en: a plan for spending money over a period of time
example: We need to stay within the budget for this project.

meaning_en: a person who applies for a job or position

meaning_en: the maximum amount something can hold or produce

meaning_en: to work jointly with others toward a common goal
example: The two departments collaborated on the product launch.

meaning_en: payment given in exchange for work or as reimbursement
example: The compensation package includes salary, bonus, and stock options.

meaning_en: able to compete successfully; involving competition

meaning_en: covering all aspects; complete and thorough

meaning_en: to establish or decide something conclusively

meaning_en: to make something easier or help it happen

meaning_en: favorable or useful for someone or something
example: Regular exercise is beneficial for employee productivity.

meaning_en: intended to be kept secret
example: All client information must be treated as strictly confidential.

meaning_en: relying on someone or something for support
example: The project timeline is dependent on supplier delivery dates.

meaning_en: possible and practical to be done with available means
example: The committee determined that the proposal was not financially feasible.

meaning_en: featuring new ideas or methods
example: The company is known for its innovative approach to product design.

meaning_en: ready to be used; relating to the day-to-day running of a business
example: The new factory will be fully operational by March.

meaning_en: awaiting resolution; about to happen
example: There are three pending applications under review.

meaning_en: occurring at regular intervals
example: Periodic audits ensure compliance with financial regulations.

meaning_en: achieving a significant amount or result
example: The meeting was productive; we agreed on all key points.

meaning_en: lasting for only a limited period of time
example: We are hiring temporary staff to cover the holiday period.

meaning_en: dependent on certain conditions being met
example: The offer is conditional upon a satisfactory background check.

meaning_en: satisfying the conditions required to be involved in something
example: Part-time employees are eligible for health benefits after six months.

meaning_en: without delay; at the correct time
example: Please respond promptly to all client emails.

meaning_en: in a way that achieves maximum output with minimum waste
example: The new system processes orders more efficiently than before.

meaning_en: in every case or on every occasion; the same way every time
example: Our products have consistently received high ratings.

meaning_en: separately, in the order already mentioned
example: The first and second prizes are worth $500 and $300 respectively.

meaning_en: regularly or habitually; often
example: The system is updated frequently to improve performance.

meaning_en: without anyone or anything in between; in a direct manner
example: Please contact the supplier directly for pricing information.

meaning_en: at first, before other things happen or are considered
example: The project was initially scheduled for Q1 but was delayed.

meaning_en: for the most part; mainly
example: The company primarily serves clients in the financial sector.

meaning_en: at the same time
example: The system can process multiple requests simultaneously.

meaning_en: for a limited period of time
example: The office will be temporarily closed for maintenance this weekend.

meaning_en: at an earlier time; before the present
example: As previously discussed, the timeline has been revised.

meaning_en: in a way that demands close adherence to rules
example: Access to the server room is strictly limited to authorized staff.

meaning_en: coming after something in time
example: The contract was signed and the project subsequently began.

meaning_en: depending on; affected by or needing to comply with something
example: All prices are subject to change without notice.

meaning_en: starting from a particular time
example: As of January 1, all expense reports must be submitted online.

meaning_en: because of; caused by
example: The delay was due to unexpected supply chain issues.

meaning_en: as well as; besides
example: In addition to salary, employees receive a performance bonus.

meaning_en: because of something; following as a consequence
example: As a result of the merger, 200 new positions were created.

meaning_en: having the duty to deal with or take care of something
example: Each team leader is responsible for their own budget.

meaning_en: with the purpose of doing or achieving something
example: In order to reduce costs, we are renegotiating supplier contracts.

meaning_en: by a specified deadline at the absolute latest
example: All submissions must be received no later than Friday at 5 PM.

meaning_en: before a particular time or event
example: Prior to the meeting, please review the attached agenda.

meaning_en: on the condition that; if
example: The discount applies provided that payment is made within 30 days.

meaning_en: to pay back money spent by someone else
example: Business travel expenses will be reimbursed within two weeks.

meaning_en: to rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
example: Currency exchange rates fluctuate constantly throughout the day.

meaning_en: to support financially, typically with public funds
example: The government subsidizes public transportation in rural areas.

meaning_en: to decrease in value over time
example: Equipment depreciates each year for accounting purposes.

meaning_en: to distribute resources or duties for a specific purpose
example: The committee allocated $50,000 for staff training this year.

meaning_en: to make previously unknown information public
example: Companies are required to disclose earnings quarterly.

meaning_en: ongoing business expenses not directly related to production
example: Rent and utilities are included in the overhead costs.

meaning_en: legal responsibility; a debt or financial obligation
example: The company's liabilities exceeded its assets last quarter.

meaning_en: ownership interest in a company; fairness
example: Employees were offered equity in the company as part of their compensation.

meaning_en: repayment of money spent by another person
example: Submit your receipts for reimbursement by the end of the month.

meaning_en: a planned route or journey; a detailed travel schedule
example: Your travel itinerary includes hotel, flight, and car rental details.

meaning_en: the act of passing through a place while traveling
example: Passengers in transit do not need to clear customs.

meaning_en: given free of charge
example: Guests receive complimentary breakfast with their room.

meaning_en: a hotel employee who assists guests with arrangements
example: The concierge arranged tickets to the show for our guests.

meaning_en: a period of rest during a journey, especially at an airport
example: We have a three-hour layover in Tokyo before the connecting flight.

meaning_en: a useful feature or service making a place more comfortable
example: The hotel offers amenities including a pool, gym, and spa.

meaning_en: the state of occupying a space; proportion of space in use
example: Hotel occupancy rates peak during the summer months.

meaning_en: the act of putting equipment into position; the equipment itself
example: Equipment installation will be completed over the weekend.

meaning_en: a first model of something from which others are developed
example: The engineering team presented the prototype at last week's meeting.

meaning_en: the range of operations that a product can perform
example: The new software offers expanded functionality for data analysis.

meaning_en: relating to cooking or the kitchen
example: The chef has extensive culinary training from Europe.

meaning_en: light food and drinks served at events
example: Refreshments will be served during the afternoon break.

meaning_en: a large formal meal for many people
example: The annual awards banquet will be held at the Grand Hotel.

meaning_en: a part or share of something; the amount of food served
example: The restaurant is known for its generous portions.

meaning_en: an arrangement to secure a seat, room, or table in advance
example: I'd like to make a reservation for four people at 7 PM.

meaning_en: to read and correct errors in a text
example: Please proofread the report before sending it to the client.

meaning_en: to discuss something formally to reach an agreement
example: We will negotiate the terms of the contract next week.

meaning_en: to gradually gather more of something over time
example: Loyalty points accumulate with every purchase.

meaning_en: an irregular rise and fall in number or amount
example: Fluctuations in raw material prices affect our production costs.

meaning_en: the process of obtaining goods or services for a business
example: The procurement department manages all vendor relationships.

meaning_en: a complete list of all goods stored or available for sale
example: Conduct a full inventory check at the end of each month.

meaning_en: a list of matters to be addressed at a formal meeting
example: Please review the agenda before attending tomorrow's meeting.

meaning_en: the degree to which a business makes profit
example: The new product line significantly improved overall profitability.

meaning_en: a desktop computer or individual work area
example: Each employee has been assigned a new ergonomic workstation.

meaning_en: the main administrative center of an organization
example: The company's headquarters are located in downtown Taipei.

meaning_en: the support given by a customer to a business
example: We thank you for your continued patronage.

meaning_en: required by law or rules; compulsory
example: Safety training is mandatory for all new employees.

meaning_en: a high level of competence or skill
example: The position requires proficiency in both English and Mandarin.

meaning_en: a person with a lower position; lower in rank
example: The manager delegated the task to a subordinate.

meaning_en: a person learning a skill under an experienced worker
example: The company hired three apprentices to train in machine operation.

meaning_en: accumulation or increase over time
example: Vacation days are credited based on accrual over the year.

meaning_en: the process of preparing and sending invoices to customers
example: All billing inquiries should be directed to the accounting department.

meaning_en: something pledged as security for repayment of a loan
example: The company used its property as collateral for the loan.

meaning_en: to make accounts consistent; to restore harmony
example: The accountant reconciled the monthly bank statements.

meaning_en: a list of employees and the wages they receive
example: New hires will be added to the payroll after their first week.
